7. Functional Programming

It’s a classic programming style heavily focused on functions. Like instead of for-loops you do recursion.

OOP is often better for modeling long-lived entities with lots of related state; FP is often better for transformations, recursion, caching, pipelines, and reducing hidden side effects.

Here’s an OOP example

7.2. @ Decorators

It just reasssigns the function to the decorated version.

7.3. Tail call Optimization

Tail call optimization is a compiler/runtime optimization where a function call that happens as the last action of a function reuses the current stack frame instead of creating a new one. This saves overhead from creating a new frame.
